article_title: Wasmer Launches Edge.js, a WebAssembly-Sandboxed Node.js Runtime Targeting AI Agent Workloads

content_preview.title: Wasmer Launches Edge.js, a WebAssembly-Sandboxed Node.js Runtime Targeting AI Agent Workloads
content_preview.summary: The open-source runtime executes Node.js v24 applications inside a WASIX sandbox without Docker, passing 99 percent of the Node.js test suite while trading 5 to 30 percent of native performance for process-level isolation.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Wasmer released Edge.js as an open-source project on March 17, 2026, introducing a JavaScript runtime that executes Node.js workloads inside a WebAssembly sandbox. The runtime targets AI agent execution and edge computing scenarios where [existing Node.js applications need to run safely without Docker containers](https://wasmer.io/posts/edgejs-safe-nodejs-using-wasm-sandbox), achieving startup times that traditional containerization cannot match.

Edge.js addresses a growing tension...
